title: <x-entity>
breadcrumbs: XHTML Elements for Static Server Pages
text: <x-entity> Uniface-specific XHTML tag that defines the location and presentation format of an entity in the layout of a static server page. <x-entity { exclude="0" | "1" } name=" EntityName "> Contents </x-entity> Arguments exclude —for compatibility with server pages created prior to Uniface 9.4. Determines whether the webgen ProcScript statement should generate the Contents in the XHTML page sent to the browser. If specified, it overwrites the exclude specification in the parent <x-entity> or <x-occurrence> . The allowed values are "0" ( Contents are generated) or "1" ( Contents are not generated). EntityName —fully qualified and capitalized name of an entity, for example, MY_ENTITY.MY_MODEL Contents —one of the following: Standard XHTML <x-subst> tags one <x-occurrence> tag, defining iteration properties for the entity <x-entity> tags for all inner entities painted on a server page Description When copying or inserting Uniface objects into the server page layout, Uniface generates <x-entity> tags for every entity you select. If you select the component and set the Document Style option to Data entry , it is automatically inserted inside a <form> element so that the data can be submitted. If you insert an entity outside a <form> tag, the data is displayed but it cannot be submitted. If the entity does not need to be sent to a browser, do not insert it in the server page. Alternatively, set the exclude attribute on the <x-entity> element to "1" , for example: <x-entity exclude="1" name="MY_ENTITY.MY_MODEL"> Copy Related Links Static Server Pages Static Server Page (USP) components generate a complete web page every time they are activated. This makes them useful for relatively static web pages or a thin-client architecture in which all processing is done on the server. They can also be used for RESTful applications or file upload functionality. Insert Uniface Objects in the Server Page Layout When creating the layout, elements in the layout must be bound to the component data structure as defined in the Component Editor. You can do this by copying the Uniface objects from the component structure and inserting them as HTML in the layout. webgen Generate an XHTML document from the layout definition and the component definition of a static server page. Layout of Static Server Pages Static server page layouts are XHTML-based and include Uniface-specific tags to define the component data structure, as well as standard XHTML for defining the layout and appearance. The structure of the layout is best viewed in the XHTML source. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
